section.title: Scope and assumptions
section.item: Goal: Rust-first iOS UI runtime with a Metal renderer and a thin UIKit shell. Zero Swift. Android deferred but anticipated by all abstractions.
Minimum iOS: 25.0. Targets: aarch64-apple-ios (device), aarch64-apple-ios-sim and x86_64-apple-ios (sim).
UIKit is used only for lifecycle, surface hosting, input/IME. All layout, state, and rendering live in Rust.
Mandatory: multithreaded command encoding, triple buffering, high‑refresh on ProMotion, full IME composition.
Security: no private APIs, no background clipboard reads, no network IO in the runtime.
section.outOfScope: Navigation, styling system, advanced widget sets beyond the base controls below, persistence, networking.

profiles.profile: debug-assertions on, overflow-checks on, panic=unwind, lto=off

features.feature: EDR/HDR path with RGBA16F targets and Display‑P3.
Enable Tokio runtime integration for background tasks.
Reserved. No implementation in this release.

dependencies.crate: objc2, icrate (UIKit, QuartzCore, Metal, Foundation, AVFoundation, CoreVideo, CoreMedia, CoreLocation, UserNotifications, Contacts, CoreBluetooth, BackgroundTasks, CoreMotion)
tracing or log (choose one; wire to env filter)

hostProject.signing: Automatic signing; capabilities are opt‑in via a generated App.entitlements.

reproducibility.item: Pin Rust crates via Cargo.lock committed.
Pin Xcode toolchain version in CI and export MTLArchive schema version in build logs.

section.title: Public API surface (stable)
section.coordinates: Positions are logical points. Multiply by scale for GPU. Pixel snapping: snap glyph and 1‑px strokes to nearest device pixel boundary after scale. See §26 Canvas normalization for aspect handling and input mapping.

errorModel.item: Use Result<T, PlatformError> and Result<T, RenderError> with exhaustive enums and Display impls.
No panics across FFI. All FFI entrypoints return int codes and log errors.

semver.item: ui-core, platform-api, renderer-api are semver’d. Breaking changes require minor/major bumps.

rules.item: ui-core depends only on platform-api and renderer-api. It must not import platform-ios or renderer-metal.
renderer-metal depends on renderer-api only; no UIKit symbols.
platform-ios owns all Objective‑C and UIKit/QuartzCore interactions.

modules.module: Widget tree, layout, event routing, draw list builder, text layout, a11y mapping.
Traits, events, units, feature flags, error enums, device caps.
Delegates, MetalView, DisplayLink, IME, a11y, clipboard, haptics, memory warnings.
Renderer trait, RenderEncoder, DrawList, resource handles.
Device/queue/pipelines, parallel encoding, atlases, present, EDR.
rustybuzz, swash, SDF/MSDF bake, atlas, bidi, grapheme clusters.
Touch/pointer/keyboard/IME mapping, Rust gesture helpers (tap, long‑press, pan).
Display link, monotonic clock, timers, frame pacing.
Arenas, ring buffers, scratch allocators, slabs.

rustEntry.item: #[no_mangle] extern "C" fn rust_entry() -> libc::c_int registers classes and calls UIApplicationMain.
Autorelease pool scopes: around UIApplicationMain and around each CADisplayLink tick callback.

sceneLifecycle.mapping: Each maps to Lifecycle events emitted to App in deterministic order.

view.item: MetalView overrides +layerClass to [CAMetalLayer class]; contentScaleFactor mirrors UIScreen.scale.

config.entry: pixelFormat = BGRA8Unorm_sRGB; RGBA16Float when ios-edr enabled and supported.
colorspace = sRGB; Display‑P3 linear when ios-edr

layout.item: On layoutSubviews: layer.drawableSize = bounds * scale; emit WindowEvent::Resized with safe insets and scale.
On traitCollectionDidChange: re‑emit Resized if scale changed; relay appearance changes if needed.

memoryPressure.item: Observe UIApplicationDidReceiveMemoryWarning; flush atlases and caches in renderer-metal and text crates.

displayLink.item: CADisplayLink targets a block. iOS 15+: preferredFrameRateRange = {{min:30, max:device_max, preferred:device_max}}.
Invalidate on resignActive; recreate on becomeActive.
When Low Power Mode is enabled, drop preferred to 60 and suspend optional background animations.

order.step: Drain UIKit input → enqueue AppEvent(s) with timestamps.
On tick: coalesce input → App::event → advance animations → layout/diff → DrawList → Renderer::begin_frame → encode_pass (parallel) → submit → present.

budget.item: If update+encode exceeds budget for 120 consecutive frames, lower preferredFrameRateRange one tier; restore after 240 stable frames.

device.item: MTLDevice from layer.device. One MTLCommandQueue for render. Optional compute queue for async uploads.
Buffers: StorageModeShared for dynamic; Textures: StorageModePrivate; Atlases in Private.

buffers.item: Triple-buffered rings for vertices/indices/uniforms with hysteresis growth (factor 1.5, max cap configurable).
Per‑worker non‑overlapping slices reserved at frame start; no CPU fences within a frame.

Ring buffers use MTLResourceOptions(StorageModeShared | HazardTrackingModeUntracked | CPUCacheModeWriteCombined); the engine guarantees no read‑after‑write within a frame; validation builds may enable tracked mode.

atlases.item: Glyph atlas: LRU paging. Staging via Shared buffer → blit to Private. Evict on memory warnings.
Image atlas: LRU with subrect updates; copy‑on‑write handles for regions.

pipelines.pipeline: SDF/MSDF; gamma‑correct compositing; per‑glyph subpixel offset.
Two-pass separable Gaussian blur (downsample chain) with configurable sigma and a tint/alpha composite.
Analytic rounded-rect fill with per-corner radii; optional stroke in a second pass.
Nine-slice image rendering using a quad grid; vertex shader expands center region without sampling distortion.
pipelines.msaa: Default off; msaa-4x toggles sample count and resolve.
pipelines.psoCache: MTLBinaryArchive persisted in Library/Application Support keyed by device+OS+features; rebuild on mismatch.

color.item: CPU colors in linear floats. Convert to sRGB on BGRA8_sRGB outputs. Keep linear Display‑P3 on RGBA16F.

frame.step: Backdrop pre-pass (conditional): if any DrawCmd::Backdrop exists, render the “below‑backdrop” sublist into an offscreen color target, then run separable blur to produce a backdrop texture.
Create MTLParallelRenderCommandEncoder for the main color pass.
Partition DrawList into W work chunks by state/texture/clip; assign to workers.
Each worker uses its child MTLRenderCommandEncoder to encode draws; Backdrop items sample the pre‑blurred texture.
End children → end parallel encoder → optional resolve → present → commit.
Attach completion handler to recycle per‑frame resources.

sync.item: No encoder shared across threads. Parallelism only via MTLParallelRenderCommandEncoder children.
Pre-pass and main pass are encoded on the SAME MTLCommandBuffer in this order: blit/compute pre-pass → parallel render pass → present. Encoder order enforces GPU execution; no GPU fence required.
If an optional async-blur uses a SECOND command buffer or compute queue, order via MTLSharedEvent: pre_cb.signal(event,v); main_cb.wait(event,v). Present from main_cb only.
Atlas uploads occur on a blit encoder before the main pass in the same command buffer; writes are visible to the main pass without extra barriers.
Ring buffers use HazardTrackingModeUntracked; per-frame slices are unique; never read from the slice written in the same frame.
Prefer a single command buffer per frame. If multiple are used, attach the completion handler to the LAST buffer to recycle per‑frame resources.
Avoid stalls: set maximumDrawableCount=3; obtain nextDrawable AFTER pre-pass encoding and immediately before starting the main render pass.

section.title: Draw list, vertex formats, encoder API

formats.item: Indices: u16 by default; auto‑upgrade to u32 when vertex count exceeds 65535.
Vertices: 2×f32 position, 2×f32 uv, 4×unorm8 color packed, optional f32 depth.

structures.code:
pub struct DrawList { pub items: Vec<DrawCmd>, }
pub enum DrawCmd { Solid { vb: VertexSpan, ib: IndexSpan, color: Color }, Image { tex: ImageHandle, dst: RectF, src: RectF, alpha: f32 }, GlyphRun { run: GlyphRun }, RRect { rect: RectF, radii: [f32; 4], color: Color }, NineSlice { tex: ImageHandle, rect: RectF, slice: Insets, alpha: f32 }, Backdrop { rect: RectF, sigma: f32, tint: Color, alpha: f32 }, Spinner { center: [f32;2], radius: f32, thickness: f32, phase: f32, alpha: f32 }, ClipPush { rect: RectI }, ClipPop, }

batching.item: Stable sort by PSO → texture → clip id; merge adjacent compatible draws.
Clip stack lowered to scissor rectangles with intersected region per nesting.
Pixel snapping for hairlines and glyph origins after scale transform.

ordering.item: All input events carry a monotonic timestamp and are delivered to App::event before draw of the same tick.
Touch/pointer order per tick: Start → Move* → End/Cancel; pointer ids are stable for a contact’s lifetime.

touch.item: InputEvent::Touch contains { id, phase:{Start,Move,End,Cancel}, x, y, pressure?, tilt?, device:{Finger,Pencil,Mouse} }.
System‑level gesture preemption maps to phase=Cancel immediately.

pointer.item: InputEvent::Pointer delivers hover/mouse movement with delta and button/modifier state where available.

gestures.item: Pinch and pan are synthesized in ui-core from raw contacts: Pinch { scale, velocity }, Pan { dx, dy, velocity }.
Double‑tap and long‑press are synthesized with platform‑neutral thresholds.

keyboard.item: InputEvent::Key { code, modifiers, phase:{Down,Repeat,Up} } with repeat cadence supplied by the platform.

textInput.item: TextEvent::Composition / Commit / SelectionChanged model IME composition uniformly (CJK, etc.).
IME show/hide reported via TextEvent::IMEShown/IMEHidden and mirrored by Platform::ime_show/ime_hide.

clipboard.item: Clipboard access is via Platform::clipboard_get/set; events are not generated for clipboard changes.

dragDrop.item: High‑level DragStart/DragOver/Drop events are exposed; platform mapping defined per backend.

container.nodeProps: label, traits, value?, hint?, activation point, frame (screen coords), enabled, selected
container.item: platform-ios creates/updates UIAccessibilityElement objects per frame; updates frames on layout/scale changes.
container.activation: Activation invokes ui-core callback to perform action.

dynamicType.item: Observe UIContentSizeCategoryDidChange; expose category to ui-core; app decides scaling policy.

focus.item: Maintain focused node id across layout changes where frame intersection remains.

section.title: Text system
section.shaping: rustybuzz with bidi and complex scripts; cache per‑font per‑size shaping plans.
section.raster: swash; SDF/MSDF bake; atlas updates batched per frame; grayscale fallback permitted.

fallback.item: Embedded fonts via manifest. If ios-coretext-fallback, query system families and extend fallback chain.

section.composition: Marked ranges drawn underlined; caret follows selection; system candidate bar is used.
section.rtl: Right‑to‑left layout supported; glyph runs honor bidi embedding levels.

tick.item: Tick order: input coalesce → App::event → layout/diff → DrawList → render submit.
Timers fire before App::event for deterministic ordering. Timer resolution ≥ 1 ms.

threadPool.item: Pool size = min(num_cpus, 6); override via env OXIDEUI_WORKERS. Work stealing enabled.

determinism.item: Provide a deterministic PRNG seed for any noncritical animations in samples; core engine must be deterministic for same input stream.

targets.item: Cold start < 600 ms to first frame on A15+.
Trivial scene ≥ 118 fps on ProMotion; ≥ 58 fps on 60 Hz devices.
No nextDrawable timeouts with triple buffering under normal load.

guidelines.item: Acquire drawable late; keep triple buffering; minimize PSO/texture switches.
Use MTLBinaryArchive to avoid PSO creation hitches; prewarm on first launch.
Flush atlases on memory warnings; rebuild lazily.

ffi.item: All Objective‑C messaging wrapped in safe Rust with documented preconditions and thread affinity.
All UIKit/QuartzCore/Metal calls on main thread unless documented thread‑safe.
Opaque handle types guard Objective‑C lifetimes. No raw pointers escape API.
No panics cross FFI. FFI functions return int codes; errors logged via tracing.

logging.item: TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR levels. Env filter OXIDEUI_LOG. Redact PII in a11y logs.

unit.item: ui-core: event routing, layout, batching determinism.
input: synthetic UITouch/UIKey shims; IME sequences for Japanese and Chinese.

snapshot.item: Readback PNGs behind snapshot-tests feature. Tolerance ≤ 1 LSB per channel.

automation.item: XCUITest for lifecycle, IME show/hide, rotation, background/foreground traversal.
Animation determinism test: seeded timeline reproduces identical keyframe snapshots across runs.
Control harness: tap/drag scripts for button/toggle/slider/scroll to verify state and performance counters.
Push token smoke: register and assert device_token callback within 10 s on device build.
BLE loopback lab test: scan for known peripheral, connect, read/write characteristic, receive notify.
Background fetch/processing: schedule BGTask in test host and verify handler invocation on device.

profiling.item: Xcode GPU Capture and Metal System Trace on device; CI stores captures for perf baselines.

deliverables.item: Device and simulator builds succeed. No Swift. One staticlib exports rust_entry.
Host app renders a test scene at device max Hz with on‑screen frame counter.
Input: multi‑touch, hover (iPad), hardware keys, IME composition, clipboard verified.
Accessibility: ≥ 5 interactive nodes traversable and activatable by VoiceOver.
Performance: 1000‑rect stress scene without present stalls; CPU < 6 ms/frame on A15.

section.title: Info.plist required keys
section.snippet: <!-- Background modes + Bluetooth usage strings --> <key>UIBackgroundModes</key> <array> <string>remote-notification</string> <string>fetch</string> <string>processing</string> <string>bluetooth-central</string> <string>bluetooth-peripheral</string> </array> <key>NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription</key> <string>Allows OxideUI to discover and connect to nearby Bluetooth devices.</string> <key>NSBluetoothPeripheralUsageDescription</key> <string>Allows OxideUI to advertise services to nearby Bluetooth devices.</string>

shaders.item: build.rs uses xcrun metal + metallib to produce default.metallib; fallback Xcode build phase allowed.
shaders.command: metal -c solid.metal -o solid.air; metallib solid.air -o default.metallib
shaders.validation: Fail build if metallib missing; embed in app bundle; path provided to renderer-metal at init.

fonts.item: Fonts under oxideui/host/ios-app/App/Fonts/ with a manifest consumed by InitContext::fonts.

resources.item: Bundle path lookup helper in platform-ios for all resources.

section.title: Android reserved (future)
section.note: Do not implement now. Keep API stable to allow drop‑in Android support later.

future.renderer: Vulkan swapchain; timeline semaphores; secondary command buffers
future.compat: Must implement the same Platform and Renderer traits and AppEvent types. ui-core unchanged.

section.title: Example code: minimal app

wp.name: platform-ios bootstrap
wp.task: Define RustAppDelegate, RustSceneDelegate, MetalView; register classes; UIApplicationMain via rust_entry; autorelease pools.
wp.ac: Scene shows clear background; deterministic lifecycle logs.

wp.name: display link and resize
wp.task: CADisplayLink wrapper with preferredFrameRateRange; resize + safe area propagation; LPM handling.
wp.ac: Frame counter at device max Hz; rotations correct; LPM drops to 60.

wp.name: renderer-metal device and PSOs
wp.task: Device/queue init; shader compile; PSOs; ring buffers; error paths.
wp.ac: Solid triangle renders; no validation errors; metallib packaged.

wp.name: parallel encoding
wp.ac: 1000 quads < 4 ms CPU on A15 test.

wp.name: input: touch + keyboard + IME
wp.task: Touch mapping; pressesBegan/Ended; UIKeyInput then UITextInput with composition; keyboard frame notifications.
wp.ac: Japanese/CJK composition verified; hardware repeat works.

wp.name: text system
wp.task: rustybuzz + swash; glyph atlas; marked text rendering; RTL paragraphs.
wp.ac: Latin and CJK paragraphs crisp at 1x–3x; RTL order correct.

wp.name: tests and profiling
wp.task: Unit + snapshot + XCUITest; GPU capture scripts; perf baselines saved.
wp.ac: CI green on device+sim; captures stored.

section.title: Acceptance tests
section.item: High‑refresh: ≥ 118 fps on ProMotion trivial scene.
No present stalls with CPU 5 ms/frame and 3 drawables.
Five concurrent touches tracked with stable ids and correct phases.
Animation: button press/release timings 80 ms down, 120 ms up within ±8 ms; toggle spring settles < 220 ms without overshoot in Reduce Motion ON.
IME: marked text displayed; commit/selection delivered; keyboard rect correct.
Hardware keys: modifiers and repeats handled.
Accessibility: rotor enumerates elements; activate triggers Rust handler.
Snapshots: 1000‑rect scene within 1 LSB tolerance to baseline.
Memory warning: atlases flushed; app remains responsive; rebuild on demand.
Push: PushToken present and stable across launches; foreground notification delivered to PushManager subscriber.
Bluetooth: discover target peripheral in ≤ 10 s; connect and round‑trip 32‑byte write→notify.
Background: BGAppRefreshTask and BGProcessingTask handlers invoked on device; tasks complete and reschedule.

section.title: Coding standards
section.rustfmt: Follow repository rustfmt.toml for Rust formatting.
section.lint: Enable clippy:pedantic for ui-core and renderer crates; CI treats warnings as errors.

section.title: Docs, licensing, CI

docs.item: Crate‑level README per crate; /docs with architecture diagrams and API guides.
Doc tests for public API examples; cargo doc published as CI artifact.

section.license: MIT or Apache‑2.0 dual; /THIRD_PARTY includes all notices.

ci.matrix: macOS latest; fixed Xcode; device tests on farm; simulator tests on iOS 18 sim.
ci.steps: build, unit, snapshot (sim), XCUITest (sim), package host, perf smoke on device.

designCanvas.size: All widget geometry authored in this canonical logical space.

fitPolicy.mode: Uniform scale to fit within safe-area bounds; center; letterbox surplus.
Uniform scale to fill and bleed for decorative layers only.

pixelSnapping.rule: Do not snap filled interior vertices; snap glyph origins only.

strokes.rule: Stroke widths specified in physical pixels; convert each frame: stroke_pt = 1 / (device_scale * s).

typography.policy: font_pt = base_pt * s * clamp(cs_mult, k_min, k_max)
typography.baseline: Align cap-height to an N‑pt baseline grid in design space before scaling.

breakpoints.range: Outside range, keep core canvas unchanged; use letterbox rails for secondary UI.

safeAreas.rule: Compute view_w/view_h from safe-area rect. Background layers may draw outside the canvas.

assets.rule: Icons via SDF/MSDF; panels via 9‑slice; avoid bitmap scaling for chrome.

section.title: Animation system
section.timebase: Monotonic from CACurrentMediaTime; engine supplies dt per tick; animations update before layout.

curves.ease: Support EaseKind values: Linear, QuadIn, QuadOut, QuadInOut, CubicIn, CubicOut, CubicInOut, BackInOut, ElasticOut, BounceOut.
curves.spring: Second‑order critically‑damped or under‑damped per SpringParams { stiffness, damping, mass, eps } with stable integration; clamp to target when |velocity| < eps and |delta| < eps.

section.properties: AnimProp: Opacity, Transform2D, ColorRGBA, CornerRadius, ShadowAlpha. Multiple props may run in parallel on the same node.

lifecycle.item: Schedule via UpdateContext::anim_start(desc). Returns AnimId. Cancel via anim_cancel or anim_cancel_prop.
Repeat: Once, Count(n), or Forever. Delay honored per desc.delay_ms.
Finish events are delivered to App::event as AppEvent::Lifecycle if requested by the caller (optional flag in AnimDesc reserved for future).

defaults.item: Default ease: CubicOut, duration=150 ms, delay=0, repeat=Once.
Respect motion reduction: if device_caps.a11y_reduce_motion, durations become 0 except essential affordances (focus, text caret).

mapping.item: ui-core applies animated values to node styles before building DrawList.
Renderer stores animated per‑instance data in ring‑buffered uniform blocks; no heap allocations per frame.

section.limits: Target ≥ 10k concurrent property animations on A15‑class devices without CPU spikes.
section.determinism: Given the same input stream and seed, results are bit‑stable across runs.

label.a11y: Static text; exposes label; no action.

button.animations: press down: Transform2D scale 0.98 for 80 ms Ease(CubicOut); release to 1.0 for 120 ms Ease(CubicOut)

zoom.gestures: pinch to zoom (scale up/down), two-finger double-tap zoom out, pan when zoomed

imageView.render: Image/NineSlice DrawCmd; zoom uses container Transform2D + clip.
imageView.a11y: Trait=image; zoom not exposed to VoiceOver.

progressBar.render: Determinate: filled track; Indeterminate: looping bar

spinner.render: DrawCmd::Spinner with phase advanced per tick

layout.grid: For Vertical axis. Cells must fit horizontal width; vertical scroll only.
layout.row: For Horizontal axis. Exactly 1 row; horizontal scroll.

collectionView.virtualization: measurement + culling; stable IDs; diff-driven updates
collectionView.a11y: Scrollable container exposes cells in order

toggle.gestures: tap toggles; drag along track snaps at 0.5
toggle.animations: knob spring to target with SpringParams { stiffness: 280.0, damping: 26.0, mass: 1.0, eps: 0.001 }

slider.gestures: drag horizontal; keyboard arrows adjust by step or 0.01

textField.props: text:String, on_change:Callback, placeholder:String?, secure:bool=false, style:{Inline, Floating}
textField.ime: Uses UITextInput; caret + marked underline. Platform keyboard shown on focus; Platform::ime_show/ime_hide available.
textField.animations: Floating: placeholder animates to label above on first input; Ease(CubicOut) 120 ms

scrollView.physics: deceleration with velocity carry; bounds clamped; overscroll glow optional
scrollView.a11y: Scrollable container; exposes children in order; adjustable actions for page up/down.

panel.render: RRect + optional shadow draw; content in ZStack above.

backdrop.render: DrawCmd::Backdrop; uses renderer backdrop pass; tint overlays blur

haptics.binding: Controls may trigger haptics on tap/commit/change via UpdateContext::haptics

acceptance.item: All controls hit‑test correctly in design space and under canvas scaling.
Button/toggle/slider respond at 120 Hz with no dropped frames in test scenes.
TextField supports selection, composition, delete, and paste; caret blinks at 0.5 Hz.
ScrollView maintains ≥ 58 fps with 1k virtualized children on A15‑class devices.
All controls expose correct accessibility traits and labels.

session.item: platform-ios creates AVCaptureSession + AVCaptureDevice for Front/Back, adds video input, sets preferred format near cfg.resolution/fps.
Use AVCaptureVideoDataOutput (BGRA) on a dedicated queue; convert CVPixelBuffer via CVMetalTextureCache to MTLTexture.
Emit CameraFrame with tex, size, timestamp_ns, rotation_deg corrected for orientation; caller composites in UI.
Stop via CameraStream::stop(); session and outputs torn down on main thread.

section.title: Permissions service

domains.domain: UNUserNotificationCenter request/observe; statuses map to PermissionStatus.
CLLocationManager requestWhenInUse; subscribe to authorizationStatus changes.
CBManagerAuthorization mapping; request via CBCentralManager on iOS; no change subscriptions.
CMMotionActivity authorization (Core Motion). Gate access by NSMotionUsageDescription; no entitlement. No change subscriptions.

section.api: Platform::permissions() → trait Permissions with request/status/subscribe; callbacks fire on main thread.
section.storage: No silent auto-requests; caller drives UX; statuses cached in memory.

section.title: Async runtime (Tokio)

model.item: platform-ios starts a Tokio multi-thread runtime on first use; threads = min(4, num_cpus).
No UI work off main thread; UI mutations dispatched back to main via a main-thread executor.

section.shutdown: Runtime lives for process lifetime; tasks must be cancellation-safe.

section.title: Bluetooth (CoreBluetooth)
section.roles: Central and Peripheral. Background eligible via UIBackgroundModes bluetooth-central / bluetooth-peripheral.

central.item: Use CBCentralManager. Start scan with service filters; deliver Discovered with RSSI and name.
Connect, discover services/characteristics lazily; reads/writes/notify routed to Bluetooth trait.
central.limits: In background, scan/connection rates are throttled by iOS; discovery payload is limited.

peripheral.item: Use CBPeripheralManager. Advertise name + service UUIDs only in background; characteristics are not in adv payload.
Handle write requests and update value for subscribed centrals.

section.threading: All CoreBluetooth delegate calls marshalled to main thread before crossing FFI.

section.title: Push notifications (APNs)

registration.item: On PushManager::register: request UNAuthorization (alert, sound, badge) then call UIApplication.registerForRemoteNotifications.
Capture device token in application:didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken: and expose via PushManager::device_token.
Report failures via didFailToRegister… to logs and a transient error.

delivery.item: Background: enable “remote‑notification” mode; content‑available=1 wakes app to process.

section.entitlements: aps-environment present in App.entitlements; value set by build configuration.

section.title: Background runtime

modes.item: Remote notifications, fetch, processing, bluetooth-central, bluetooth-peripheral enabled.

bgTasks.item: Use BGTaskScheduler for refresh (BGAppRefreshTask) and processing (BGProcessingTask). Add identifiers under BGTaskSchedulerPermittedIdentifiers in Info.plist.
platform-ios exposes register_bg_task(id, kind) and schedule_bg_task(id, earliest_ms) helpers.
All BG task handlers must call setTaskCompleted(success:) and schedule next run.

section.lifecycle: When entering background, renderer pauses; network or BLE work continues only if an eligible mode is active.

section.title: Host capability manifest and codegen
section.goal: Only ship entitlements and usage descriptions that the host app opts into.
section.manifest:
# capabilities.toml [usage_strings] NSCameraUsageDescription = "Needed to scan documents." NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription = "Connect to nearby accessories." NSBluetoothPeripheralUsageDescription = "Advertise to nearby accessories." NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription = "Show nearby devices." NSLocationAlwaysAndWhenInUseUsageDescription = "Enable geofenced features." NSContactsUsageDescription = "Invite friends from contacts." NSMotionUsageDescription = "Enable motion-based interactions."
[entitlements] push_notifications = false bluetooth_central = false bluetooth_peripheral = false background_fetch = false background_remote_notification = false background_processing = false location = "none" # "none" | "when_in_use" | "always"

codegen.step: Generates/merges App.entitlements with: - aps-environment = "development" when push_notifications=true (CI can swap to "production"). - com.apple.developer.background-modes array entries per background_* flags. - Bluetooth roles per bluetooth_* flags.
Generates an Info.plist fragment adding only the nonempty usage strings above; merges into App/Info.plist by keys.
Errors if a required usage string is missing for an enabled domain (e.g., camera=true but no NSCameraUsageDescription).

build.item: CI step runs `cargo xtask ios prepare` before Xcode build; artifacts: App.entitlements, Info.plist merged.

runtimeGuards.item: platform-ios compiles all bridges; APIs return PlatformError::CapabilityDisabled if the host did not opt in via manifest.

acceptance.item: With empty manifest, no extra usage keys or background modes appear; app passes App Store validation for capabilities.
Enabling each flag injects exactly the corresponding keys; no unrelated keys present.

touch.item: Override touchesBegan/Moved/Ended/Cancelled; map UITouch to InputEvent::Touch with id, phase, x, y, force, altitude/azimuth (pencil).
System gesture steals produce Cancelled immediately.

hover.item: UIHoverGestureRecognizer supplies hover deltas; UIPointerInteraction optional for visuals only.

gestures.item: UIPinchGestureRecognizer → Pinch; UIPanGestureRecognizer → Pan; two‑finger double‑tap → zoom out.

hardwareKeyboard.item: pressesBegan/Changed/Ended map UIKey to KeyEvent with modifiers; repeat cadence matches UIKit.

textInput.item: UITextInput implements selection/marked text → Composition and SelectionChanged; keyboardWillChangeFrame → IMEShown/IMEHidden.

clipboard.item: UIPasteboard general pasteboard for UTF‑8 strings via Platform::clipboard_get/set.

dragDrop.item: UIDragInteraction / UIDropInteraction on MetalView mapped to high‑level events.

layout.item: Choreographer for frame timing; mirrored to timing crate.

permissions.item: Placeholders only; no Android manifest keys generated in this release.

mapping.item: Input events map to the same InputEvent / TextEvent enums; IME via AInputMethod (TBD).
